title: RuntimeTuning
description: Per-load tuning DTO for the Local-LLM extension's llama-server adapter. Mirrors the Rust struct at extensions/builtin/local-llm/rust/src/chat/handlers.rs::RuntimeTuning and the TS interface at apps/web/src/services/local_llm_chat.ts::RuntimeTuning. Spec 039 adds 10 optional fields (cache_reuse, cram_mb, checkpoint_every_n_tokens, n_cpu_moe, min_p, dry_multiplier, dry_base, dry_allowed_length, dry_penalty_last_n, swa_full).

cache_reuse.description: When set, llama-server runs with --cache-reuse N. Default form value when toggle on: 256.

cram_mb.description: When set, llama-server runs with --cram MB. Default form value when toggle on: 1024.

checkpoint_every_n_tokens.description: When set, llama-server runs with --checkpoint-every-n-tokens N. Default form value when toggle on: 8192.

n_cpu_moe.description: When set and > 0, llama-server runs with --n-cpu-moe N. Some(0) and None both treated as off (no flag emitted). Form-emitted values are bounded by AvailableModel.expert_layer_count (or 64 fallback when unknown); the schema's 256 cap is a permissive backstop for direct API callers — no released model has more transformer blocks than this.

min_p.description: When > 0.0, llama-server runs with --min-p p. Some(0.0) suppresses the flag. Default per Creative preset: 0.02; per Code preset: 0.10.

dry_multiplier.description: When > 0.0, llama-server runs with --dry-multiplier m AND emits the rest of the DRY quartet. Some(0.0) suppresses ALL DRY flags. Creative preset default: 0.8.

dry_base.description: Emitted with --dry-base only when dry_multiplier is positive. Creative preset default: 1.75.

dry_allowed_length.description: Emitted with --dry-allowed-length only when dry_multiplier is positive. Creative preset default: 2.

dry_penalty_last_n.description: Emitted with --dry-penalty-last-n only when dry_multiplier is positive. -1 means use ctx_size.

swa_full.description: When true, llama-server runs with --swa-full. Set automatically by the form when cache_reuse override is on for known-broken families (FR-029).
